county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Jefferson County formed 1839 from Henry. County seat is Fairfield.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Fairfield holds deeds from 1841; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Jefferson County was an early southeastern Iowa settlement area. Fairfield has been home to Parsons College (closed 1973) and later the Maharishi University (Transcendental Meditation community, est. 1973). Jefferson County Historical Society in Fairfield. Strong Quaker influence -- Fairfield Friends Meeting. Libertyville and Batavia communities. Skunk River valley. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Jefferson County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.source_name: Jefferson County Deed Records -- County Recorder
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Fairfield
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books, mortgages, and land records from 1839 to present. County Recorder maintains grantor/grantee indexes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.source_name: Jefferson County Probate Records -- Clerk of District Court
source_profiles.repository: Clerk of District Court, Fairfield
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1839. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.source_name: Jefferson County Marriage Records
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Fairfield / Iowa Department of Public Health
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1839. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Jefferson County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents (homesteads, cash sales, military warrants) for Jefferson County. Iowa is a public land state; most land was transferred from federal ownership between 1838 and 1870.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records complete; originals at NARA

source_profiles.source_name: Jefferson County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1840-1950 for Jefferson County. Iowa territorial census 1836, 1838; Iowa state census 1847, 1849, 1851-1925 (every 2 years). Agricultural schedules available.

source_profiles.source_name: Jefferson County Cemetery Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / Jefferson County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Jefferson County. Township cemeteries, church cemeteries, and city cemeteries. Iowa Genealogical Society and county historical societies have published transcription volumes. Find A Grave has extensive Iowa coverage.

source_profiles.source_name: Jefferson County Tax Lists and Assessment Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Jefferson County Auditor, Fairfield
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Jefferson County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

source_profiles.source_name: Jefferson County Quaker (Friends) Meeting Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa Yearly Meeting / Swarthmore College Friends Historical Library / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Friends meeting records including minutes, membership lists, certificates of removal, birth/death/marriage registers. Jefferson County Quaker meetings were active in the Underground Railroad.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Johnson County formed 1837 from Dubuque (Indian Cession). County seat is Iowa City.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Iowa City holds deeds from 1839; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Johnson County is home to Iowa City, the first capital of Iowa Territory (1841-1857) and seat of the University of Iowa (est. 1847). State Historical Society of Iowa main research library is in Iowa City. The Old Capitol building is preserved on the university campus. Johnson County Historical Society. University of Iowa Libraries Special Collections hold extensive Iowa manuscripts. Czech settlers in Solon and Shueyville. Coralville and North Liberty communities. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Johnson County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1837. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Jones County formed 1837 from Dubuque (Indian Cession). County seat is Anamosa.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Anamosa holds deeds from 1839; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Jones County is home to the Anamosa State Penitentiary (est. 1872) -- inmate records at Iowa State Archives document individuals statewide. Grant Wood, the artist, was from Anamosa. Grant Wood Art Gallery. Jones County Historical Society. Monticello and Olin communities. Wapsipinicon River valley. Stone City art colony site. Edinburgh area Scotch-Irish settlement. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Jones County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Keokuk County formed 1843 from Washington. County seat is Sigourney.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Sigourney holds deeds from 1844; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Keokuk County was named for Chief Keokuk of the Sauk people. Settled by migrants from Ohio, Indiana, and Virginia in the 1840s. What Cheer was a coal mining town -- mining community records. Keokuk County Historical Society. South English and Richland communities. Skunk River and North Skunk River valleys. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Keokuk County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Kossuth County formed 1851 from Indian lands. County seat is Algona.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Algona holds deeds from 1857; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Kossuth County is Iowa's largest county by area. Named for Hungarian patriot Lajos Kossuth. Settled in the 1870s-1880s after drainage of the prairie wetlands. Algona was the site of a WWII German POW camp (Camp Algona, 1943-1946) -- prisoner and guard records at NARA. Kossuth County Historical Society. German and Scandinavian settlers. Bancroft, Swea City, and Titonka communities. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Kossuth County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Lee County formed 1836 from Des Moines. County seat is Fort Madison.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Fort Madison holds deeds from 1836; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Lee County is at Iowa's southeastern tip where the Des Moines and Mississippi Rivers meet. Fort Madison (1808) and Keokuk are among Iowa's oldest communities. The Half-Breed Tract (1824-1850s) between the two rivers has unique land title records due to Congress granting land to children of mixed Native American and European ancestry. Lee County Historical Society. Iowa State Penitentiary in Fort Madison (est. 1839) -- inmate records at State Archives. Keokuk Medical College records. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Lee County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1836. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1836. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Linn County formed 1837 from Dubuque (Indian Cession). County seat is Cedar Rapids.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Cedar Rapids holds deeds from 1839; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Linn County is Iowa's second most populous county, centered on Cedar Rapids. The Czech and Slovak community in Cedar Rapids is nationally significant -- National Czech & Slovak Museum & Library holds family research collections, church records, and fraternal (ZCBJ/Sokol) records. Coe College archives. Linn County Historical Society and Carl & Mary Koehler History Center. Marion and Mount Vernon communities. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Linn County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Louisa County formed 1836 from Des Moines. County seat is Wapello.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Wapello (the town) holds deeds from 1837; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Louisa County is one of the earliest Iowa counties. Wapello, Columbus Junction, and Morning Sun communities. The Iowa River joins the Mississippi in this county. Swedish settlers in the Lockridge-Morning Sun area. Louisa County Historical Society. Toolesboro mound group (prehistoric). Columbus City was a river port. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Louisa County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Lucas County formed 1846 from Unorganized territory. County seat is Chariton.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Chariton holds deeds from 1849; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Lucas County was a major coal mining region. Coal miners from England, Wales, Scotland, and Sweden settled in the 1870s-1890s. John L. Lewis, United Mine Workers president, was from Lucas. Lucas County Historical Society in Chariton. Stephens State Forest area. Chariton River valley. Mining town records at Iowa State Archives. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Lucas County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.source_name: Lucas County Deed Records -- County Recorder
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Chariton
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books, mortgages, and land records from 1846 to present. County Recorder maintains grantor/grantee indexes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.source_name: Lucas County Probate Records -- Clerk of District Court
source_profiles.repository: Clerk of District Court, Chariton
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1846. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.source_name: Lucas County Marriage Records
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Chariton / Iowa Department of Public Health
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1846. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Lucas County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents (homesteads, cash sales, military warrants) for Lucas County. Iowa is a public land state; most land was transferred from federal ownership between 1838 and 1870.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records complete; originals at NARA

source_profiles.source_name: Lucas County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1850-1950 for Lucas County. Iowa territorial census 1836, 1838; Iowa state census 1847, 1849, 1851-1925 (every 2 years). Agricultural schedules available.

source_profiles.source_name: Lucas County Cemetery Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / Lucas County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Lucas County. Township cemeteries, church cemeteries, and city cemeteries. Iowa Genealogical Society and county historical societies have published transcription volumes. Find A Grave has extensive Iowa coverage.

source_profiles.source_name: Lucas County Tax Lists and Assessment Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Lucas County Auditor, Chariton
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Lucas County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

source_profiles.source_name: Lucas County Mining Records and Mine Inspector Reports
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / State Historical Society of Iowa
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Mine inspector reports, coal company records, mine accident reports, and related court records for Lucas County coal mining communities. Iowa Mine Inspector annual reports list mines, operators, and sometimes workers.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Lyon County formed 1851 from Indian lands. County seat is Rock Rapids.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Rock Rapids holds deeds from 1872; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Lyon County in Iowa's northwest corner was settled late (1870s) by Dutch, German, and Scandinavian immigrants. Dutch Reformed church records are important. Blood Run National Historic Landmark (Oneota/Ioway archaeological site). Lyon County Historical Society. George, Doon, and Larchwood communities. Rock River valley. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Lyon County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.repository: Luther College Archives, Decorah / ELCA Archives / FamilySearch
